utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items requested in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all 5 items are present. Now check if each is fully specified with all details as required: (1) health_coverage requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan includes both physical_health_cost and mental_health_cost and a total_cost; thus fully specified. (2) property_coverage requires home/contents insurance with costs. The plan provides a plan name, a monthly cost, and benefits; thus specified. (3) travel_coverage requires travel insurance with costs. The plan provides a plan name, a monthly cost, and benefits; thus specified. (4) additional_services requires any enhancement services with costs. The plan lists enhancement services but does not provide explicit costs for these services; they are described as included within other plans without distinct costs. However, since the format asks for any enhancement services with costs, inclusion without separate costs may still be acceptable if the costs are effectively included in parent plans. Given the phrasing 'with costs,' the absence of explicit cost allocation for additional services could be considered incomplete. (5) total_budget requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown; monthly and annual totals are provided. Therefore, 4 items are fully specified; additional_services lacks explicit costs.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes three items: PrimeCare Premium Plan (health), EliteHome Premium Coverage (property), and PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel). I located these exact names in the ground-truth ratings under primary_health_coverage (physical and mental health listing PrimeCare Premium Plan), property_protection (home_content listing EliteHome Premium Coverage), and mobility_coverage (travel_insurance listing PrimeTravel Premium Plan). I then extracted their corresponding ratings: PrimeCare Premium Plan has rating 6 for physical health and 4 for mental health; EliteHome Premium Coverage has rating 6; PrimeTravel Premium Plan has rating 6. Since the final plan lists PrimeCare Premium Plan for both physical and mental health, both ratings should be provided.
